title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government has responded to a 2020 report on artificial intelligence and public standards by outlining its approach to the emerging technology. The response will likely cover how the government plans to address concerns and recommendations made in the original report.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This report examines the use of copyrighted materials in artificial intelligence, assessing its potential impact on copyright law. It likely investigates how AI systems rely on copyrighted works and the implications for creators and users.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education is reviewing the use of generative artificial intelligence in education to ensure it enhances learning and teaching. The purpose is to provide guidance on how generative AI can be safely and effectively used in educational settings.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: This transport AI action plan outlines the steps the Department for Transport (DfT) will take to work with the industry to develop and safely use artificial intelligence in transportation. The plan aims to balance the benefits and risks of AI in transport.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This document outlines the progress of using copyrighted materials in artificial intelligence system development as per the Data (Use and Access) Act. It appears to discuss the legal implications and regulation of AI development that incorporates copyrighted content.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The UK government has responded to a report from the Regulatory Horizons Council, outlining their intentions for regulating AI used as medical devices. The government's response addresses the use of artificial intelligence in healthcare and aims to ensure patient safety.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: This document outlines Ofqual's approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in educational qualifications. It responds to a request from government officials to establish guidelines for AI in the qualifications sector.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has released a report in response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence, addressing concerns and providing a plan to address the committee's recommendations. The report likely outlines steps to regulate and utilize artificial intelligence effectively in the UK.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the terms of reference and frequently asked questions for a committee's review of artificial intelligence in relation to public standards. The review aims to examine how AI aligns with and impacts public standards.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The Centre for Science and Policy (CSPL) submitted evidence to an inquiry on the governance of artificial intelligence. This submission is part of the Science and Technology Committee's investigation into the topic.
